expectations.evidence: Script loads anthropic.mplstyle directly: plt.style.use("/Users/yulong/.config/matplotlib/stylelib/anthropic.mplstyle") on line 14
PNG file exists at /Users/yulong/code/dotfiles/claude/skills/anthropic-style-workspace/iteration-2/bar-chart/without_skill/outputs/mmlu_benchmark_chart.png and is readable as an image

Script defines PRETTY_CYCLE with exact Anthropic colors: BOOK_CLOTH = "#B86046", GREY = "#656565", DARK_BLUE = "#40668C", KRAFT = "#D19B75" (lines 7-11). Chart bars use these four colors in order: orange, grey, dark blue, tan
Chart does not use default matplotlib blue (#1f77b4). All bar colors are from PRETTY_CYCLE (B86046, 656565, 40668C, D19B75). Dark blue bar is #40668C (Anthropic DARK_BLUE), not matplotlib default
Script uses yerr parameter in bar call (line 25: ax.bar(..., yerr=errors, ...) with errors=[1.2, 0.9, 1.5, 1.1]). Chart visually shows error bars on all four bars with dark caps

Annotations use fontweight="medium" (line 44), not normal weight. Script specifies fontweight="medium" in the text call, which is heavier than normal (normal weight would be fontweight="normal" or omitted)
